pattern: Track running even-index and odd-index sums on both sides of a candidate removal, remembering that everything after the removed slot shifts parity by one.
hints: Deleting an index doesn't just remove its value: every element after it slides one position left, so its parity in the resulting array flips.
Precompute the total sum of values sitting at even positions and at odd positions, then sweep the array maintaining running even/odd sums for the part strictly before the current index.

At index i, the part after i keeps its original parity sums (derivable from the totals minus the prefix through i); because those elements shift parity, the post-removal even sum is prefix-even plus suffix-odd, and the post-removal odd sum is prefix-odd plus suffix-even. Count indices where those two match.

edge_inputs.description: single element, removing it always leaves an empty (fair) array
strictly increasing values, no shortcut symmetry
values arranged so only interior indices work, not the ends
values chosen so no index makes the array fair

stress.description: random values across the constraint range at a large size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [arr]
stress.note: plain uniform random exercises the running-sum bookkeeping at scale
